commentaries.author: Ambrose of Milan
commentaries.content: By this he makes it clear that there are other apostles besides those eleven.

commentaries.author: Basil the Great
commentaries.content: We believe and confess that, rising on the third day from the dead, according to the Scriptures, he was seen by his holy disciples and others, as it is written. He ascended into heaven and sits on the right hand of the Father when he will come at the end of time to raise up all men and to render to each according to his works. .

commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: After that He was seen of James. The son of Alphæus, first Bishop of Jerusalem, and styled brother of the Lord. There is a tradition mentioned by Jerome (Lib. in Jacobo) that James had taken a vow not to eat anything till he should see Christ risen. Jerome, however, does not think the tradition of any value. Its falsity is seen, too, (1. ) for it is evident, from this passage of S. Paul, that Christ appeared to him after appearing to the five hundred brethren, and therefore long after His resurrection, too long for S. James"s fast to have been prolonged naturally. ) All the Apostles, and therefore S. James , were confounded at Christ"s death, and did not believe in His resurrection. It is not likely then that James would take such a vow. Jerome says that he took this story from the "Gospel according to the Hebrews ," which is apocryphal. It is also said there that Christ wore at the time a linen garment, and that He gave it to the servant of the priest, which also seems false; for the garments of Christ remained in the sepulchre (S. ), and a glorified body, such as Christ"s was, is not clad with linen or any such garments, but with splendour and rays of light. Then of all the apostles, and the disciples as well, says S. Anselm, at the ascension.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: He was seen by James. The time is not mentioned in the gospels. (Witham)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: This must be James, the Lord’s brother, whom he ordained as the first bishop of Jerusalem. The apostles mentioned here would include the seventy and others besides the Twelve.
